Ad Text:
Senior Information Management Architect with a Major Federal Agency



Your new company
Emposo is a specialist technology and capability consulting firm partnering with federal government agencies to deliver complex transformation programs. Known for its collaborative delivery model and focus on knowledge transfer, Emposo embeds expert consultants alongside client teams to deliver meaningful and sustainable outcomes.


Your new role
As the Senior Information Management Consultant, you will lead the delivery of a major Information Management Uplift Program for a prominent Canberra-based federal agency. You'll drive the enterprise information architecture design, guide co‑design workshops across multiple business areas, and oversee the pilot program shaping SharePoint Online, Purview governance and metadata standards. Acting as Emposo's primary delivery lead, you'll ensure cohesion across technical, policy and change workstreams and maintain strong engagement with project governance stakeholders.


What you'll need to succeed

8+ years delivering enterprise IM or Information Architecture programs within APS or federal government environments
Strong stakeholder management capability, including working with SES Band 1/2
Deep expertise in SharePoint Online IA (taxonomy, metadata, content types, hub sites, governance models)
Experience leading co‑design workshops with non‑technical business stakeholders
Familiarity with PSPF requirements, OFFICIAL:Sensitive handling, Digital Continuity 2025 and Archives Act compliance
Ability to guide technical specialists and translate architecture decisions into clear technical requirements
Light delivery management capability, including risk/issue tracking and acceptance‑criteria governance
NV1 clearance or ability to obtain one prior to commencement
What you'll get in return

Competitive contract rate
High‑impact role shaping a whole‑of‑agency IM uplift with executive visibility
A genuinely collaborative delivery environment working across IM, cyber, legal, governance and architecture teams
Opportunity to create enterprise‑level IA standards, patterns and reusable templates that will influence long‑term agency adoption
Supportive Emposo consulting environment with a strong focus on quality and shared success




If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

Job Title: Executive Liability Loss Adjuster – Lead, Influence & Drive Market Impact (Syd
Ad Text:
Crawford & Company is seeking an accomplished Senior / Executive Liability Loss Adjuster to lead high-value, complex liability claims and act as the senior technical and market authority for the local liability practice. This is a unique opportunity to combine hands-on claims management with visible leadership, market engagement, and business development responsibilities within a globally recognised claims organisation.

The Role:

Manage a portfolio of complex, high-value liability claims from appointment through settlement
Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team, fostering technical excellence, collaboration, and knowledge sharing
Represent Crawford as a senior technical authority and visible market leader
Contribute to business development and the strategic growth of the practice
Demonstrate leadership potential with the aspiration and capability to progress into broader departmental or practice leadership

About You:

Senior liability adjuster with 5–10+ years’ experience managing complex claims
Established and credible presence in the insurance market with strong professional relationships
Proven leadership experience and clear aspiration to grow a high-performing team
Chartered Loss Adjuster accreditation highly desirable
Exceptional stakeholder management, negotiation, and conflict resolution skills

This is a career-defining opportunity for an experienced adjuster seeking strategic influence, leadership visibility, and ongoing professional development, with the opportunity to shape the future of the liability practice.

We welcome applications from talented candidates worldwide with the skills and experience to succeed in the role. Visa sponsorship and relocation support may be available for exceptional applicants. All successful candidates will be required to complete a national police check.

Job Title: Cluster Lead JAPAC, Accounts and Customer Growth for Global Advisory Services
Ad Text:
Cluster Lead – Data, Technology, and Advisory Services (Senior Leadership Role)

Location: Singapore, Tokyo, or Sydney
Function: Advisory Services, Global Data, Technology, and Advisory Services (DTAS)

About the Role

We are seeking inspirational leaders to shape and elevate our Global enterprise Data, Technology, and Advisory Services presence across the United States, Japan, and Asia/Pacific. In an increasingly complex market, our HQ, regional HQ, and enterprise clients face deep challenges. Cluster Leads will serve as trusted partners to these clients, developing deep understanding of their challenges and consistently earning IQVIA the opportunity to support them.

Globally, IQVIA’s clients face daunting challenges: declining R&D productivity, relentless cost pressure, and deteriorating operating economics. Clients urgently need to evolve their models to achieve greater agility, speed, and commercial precision. By combining its unique proprietary data assets, its deep expertise in data architecture and analytics, and its global advisory services, IQVIA has a unique opportunity to be a partner of choice to clients as they navigate this transformation. Earning this opportunity requires strong front-line leaders with a proven ability to build intimacy and trust with senior decision-makers.

As the Cluster Lead for JAPAC, you will serve as the senior client-facing DTAS leader for your region, shaping commercial strategy, driving growth, and ensuring exceptional delivery across a cross-functional team. You will act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, deepen client intimacy, and champion integrated solutions across a range of asset and above-asset priorities. You will bring a partner-level presence, demonstrating the strategic influence, commercial acumen, and people leadership central to the success of professional service firms.

What You’ll Do:

Be the senior face of DTAS for your Cluster, trusted by SVP‑level and other senior stakeholders as a strategic advisor, sounding board, and thought‑partner
Set and own the Technology and Advisory Services growth agenda for your cluster — defining key client priorities, shaping large‑scale opportunities, and driving year‑over‑year expansion and customer satisfaction
Model deep client intimacy and exemplify best‑in‑class client leadership, operating close to your customers, regularly present onsite, and deeply embedded in their strategic priorities
Facilitate cross‑functional collaboration to leverage expertise within Advisory Services (brand strategy, portfolio strategy, value & access, digital health) and the broader DTAS portfolio (data strategy, architecture, outsourcing, technology) — fostering a culture of excellence, collaboration, and innovation.
Ensure high-impact delivery, ensuring work is insight‑rich, strategic, rigorous, and reflective of the high expectations of our customers; further, you will work with your peers across DTAS to ensure a consistent and high standard of deliverable quality and customer experience
Cultivate and nurture top talent, coaching and elevating emerging leaders, providing crisp direction, and creating an environment where teams thrive and grow.
Inform and deepen IQVIA’s Thought Leadership and insight, collaborating with IQVIA’s Thought Leadership organization and front-line practitioners to shape, refine, and communicate strategic insights about critical issues facing our clients
Influence across the enterprise, collaborating closely with other DTAS leaders, GADs, Client Partners, and senior executives to lead and shape client engagement on priority topics and accelerate impact
Own your business, delivering on customer revenue and margin targets

What You Bring

Experience operating at Senior Principal or Partner level in a top-tier consulting environment or comparable internal strategy/advisory leadership.
Broad capabilities and experience across key client priorities, including technology, transformation, commercial strategy, and health care.
A track record of owning and growing major client relationships, with deep comfort engaging senior decision-makers.
Strong commercial acumen: opportunity shaping, complex pursuit leadership, customer value articulation, senior stakeholder orchestration.
A leadership style that inspires confidence, unlocks team performance, and elevates the bar for consulting excellence.
Ability to lead, inspire, and develop multi-disciplinary teams in a dynamic, evolving environment.
Passion for client engagement and a pronounced ability to operate with high presence on client site, building trust through proximity, responsiveness, and insight.
Deep intellectual curiosity and learning agility: a pronounced ability to learn and digest new content and to apply it to customer challenges and priorities
Exceptional communication, storytelling, and influence skills — able to simplify, inspire, and drive alignment across diverse groups.

IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com

IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

About the Role
Our client is seeking a Senior Investment Operations Performance Analyst to take ownership of the preparation, quality assurance and delivery of critical investment performance reports. This role plays a key part in uplifting the reporting ecosystem through automation, data integration, and advanced analytics, making it an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys blending technical capability with investment insights.

Key Responsibilities
Prepare and validate daily, weekly and monthly investment performance reports
Collaborate with custodians and data providers to ensure accuracy and consistency
Analyse performance drivers and produce clear commentary for stakeholders
Enhance reporting through automation, BI tools and AI integrations
Support cross‑training across performance, unit pricing and regulatory reporting
About You
Degree in Finance, Commerce, Economics or similar
5–10 years’ investment performance analytics experience in super or funds management
Strong ETL, SQL and API integration skills
Proficiency with Python or similar for data workflows
Experienced using BI tools (Power BI/Tableau) and performance engines (e.g., FactSet, MSCI)
About the Company
Our client is a well‑established Australian financial services organisation with national reach. They operate within a complex, data‑rich investment environment and are known for their commitment to innovation, member outcomes, and continuous improvement. With a collaborative structure and supportive leadership, they offer opportunities to influence systems, enhance reporting capability and contribute to organisational uplift.

For more information or a confidential discussion, please apply.

Job Title: Business Intelligence Developer - Single Digital Patient Record
Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Position: Business Intelligence Developer (multiple roles) 
Award: Health Manager Level 3
Employment Type: Permanent Full-Time

Opportunity to work from our modern Chatswood/St Leonards office
Attractive salary from $132,236 up to $150,222 + 12% Super and annual leave loading
Allocated day off per month, salary packaging, discounted fitness passport and more!
Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation

Applications Close: 11:59pm, 26 March 2026


About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state. 

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere. 

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day.
 

About the Opportunity
As a Business Intelligence Developer within our Single Digital Patient Record Implementation Authority (SDPRIA) team, you will help make a real difference in healthcare through the design, development, and support of high-quality analytics tools and reporting solutions used by Local Health Districts and the NSW Ministry of Health. Your work will ensure critical health data is transformed into actionable insights to inform key decisions across the health system. 

This role offers the opportunity to contribute to a range of complex and meaningful analytics projects that support the delivery and improvement of public health services. You’ll work with large-scale health datasets and tools such as SQL, SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS), and business intelligence platforms on Windows. 

In this role, you will:

Develop and maintain responsive, reliable, and maintainable dashboards, reports, extracts, and analytics tools
Analyse user requirements and provide tailored technical solutions to meet business needs
Lead support and incident management activities across SDPR platforms, ensuring high performance and service quality
Implement performance testing and manage change processes for reporting and analytics solutions
Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver easy-to-use, plain language tools that enhance understanding and use of health data 

About You

We are looking for someone who:

Has advanced SQL skills and experience in a large, complex service industry environment
Brings extensive knowledge of complex data models and demonstrated experience working with large and complex databases
Is experienced with analytics tools, SSIS, data warehousing, ETL processes and writing custom SQL queries for reporting and dashboards.
Holds a relevant qualification in Computer Science or a related field
Can work both independently and collaboratively, demonstrating resilience and problem-solving under pressure.
Has experience with version control, change management processes, and requirement gathering for reporting solutions.
Provides technical consultation on upgrades, installations, and modifications related to performance testing and analytics tools.

 

You will succeed in this role if you:

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same.
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go.
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused.
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast moving space. 

Need more information?

Position description
Check out our diversity and inclusion commitment
Learn more about Single Digital Patient Record

Working at SDPR

Our people are at the heart of who we are. We support our people with great benefits so they can enrich health in millions of ways every day:

Professional growth and career development
Allocated day off per month
Salary packaging
Various health and wellbeing programs to support our team
 

How to apply

Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience. 

We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we accept their use, we’re eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application! 

Role-specific enquiries: If you have any questions about the role, please contact the hiring Andrew Ponce at Andrew.Ponce@health.nsw.gov.au 

General recruitment and adjustment enquiries: If you require assistance, have any questions about the recruitment or require adjustments to the recruitment/interview process, please contact Vaaruni Narayana at Vaaruni.narayanaiyengar@health.nsw.gov.au


Additional information 

This role may require evening, night, weekend and/or shift work, in accordance with operational needs and Award provisions.
This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months.
To be eligible for this role you must have current Australian work rights (Australian citizen, permanent resident, New Zealand citizen with a current passport, or hold a valid visa with permission to work in Australia).
Successful candidates may be required to multiple virtual Epic training sessions and Epic orientation, all scheduled in U.S. time.
